fields.name: Wapsipinicon Peach
fields.description: A distinctive pale yellow tomato with softly fuzzy skin, often described as peach-like. Fruits are mild, sweet and unusual, making it a memorable variety for salads, tasting tables and seed-saving displays.

fields.name: Golden Sunrise
fields.description: A golden-yellow heritage tomato with medium-sized fruits and a mild, sweet flavour. Its bright colour makes it useful in mixed tomato salads and helps show the colour range available beyond standard red varieties.

fields.name: Klondike
fields.description: An orange beefsteak-style tomato producing warm-coloured fruits with a mild, sweet flavour. A useful variety for adding orange colour to the Seed Library and for showing how heritage tomatoes can vary in both shape and tone.

fields.name: Chadwick Cherry
fields.description: A vigorous heirloom cherry tomato named after organic gardening teacher Alan Chadwick. Plants produce generous clusters of bright red cherry tomatoes with a balanced sweet-tart flavour. A useful choice for salads, snacking and steady greenhouse harvests.

fields.name: Italian Spaghetti
fields.description: An elongated red paste tomato with dense flesh and a distinctive cigar-like shape. Useful for sauces, bottling, drying and cooking, while still being interesting enough to display as a heritage curiosity.

fields.name: Ailsa Craig
fields.description: A classic British heritage tomato with neat, round red fruits and a reliable old-fashioned flavour. A useful all-rounder for salads, slicing and greenhouse growing.

fields.name: Amish Paste
fields.description: A large paste tomato valued for sauces, passata and preserving. Fruits are meaty with fewer seeds than many slicers, while still having enough flavour for fresh use.

fields.name: Aunt Ruby's German Green
fields.description: A green-when-ripe beefsteak with a rich, sweet-tart flavour. Ripe fruits soften and develop a warm yellow-green blush, making it a useful variety for showing colour diversity.

fields.name: Black Cherry
fields.description: A dark cherry tomato with mahogany-red fruits and a deep, sweet flavour. Productive trusses make it useful for snacking, salads and showing that heritage tomatoes are not only large beefsteaks.

fields.name: Black Krim
fields.description: A famous dark beefsteak tomato with dusky red-brown fruits, green shoulders and a rich savoury flavour. Often grown for its dramatic colour and strong taste.

fields.name: Brandywine
fields.origin: United States, introduced in the late 19th century
fields.description: One of the best-known heirloom beefsteak tomatoes, valued for large fruits and excellent flavour. A classic choice for slicing, sandwiches and heritage tomato displays.

fields.name: Cherokee Purple
fields.description: A celebrated heirloom beefsteak with dusky rose-purple fruits and sweet, complex flavour. Its unusual colour and story make it a favourite among seed savers.

fields.name: Costoluto Genovese
fields.description: A deeply ribbed Italian tomato with dramatic, fluted fruits. Traditionally used for slicing and sauce, it brings a distinctive heritage shape to the Seed Library.

fields.name: Dwarf Champion
fields.origin: United States, 19th century dwarf tomato line
fields.description: A compact historic dwarf tomato suited to smaller gardens and container growing. Useful for showing that heritage varieties can include space-saving plant forms as well as tall cordons.

fields.name: Gardener's Delight
fields.description: A classic cherry tomato widely grown by home gardeners for sweet, bite-sized red fruits. A useful reliable variety for beginners and greenhouse growers.

fields.name: Green Zebra
fields.description: A striking green tomato with yellow-green striping when ripe. Tangy, bright and decorative, it helps demonstrate the colour diversity of non-supermarket tomatoes.

fields.name: Jaune Flammée
fields.description: A French orange tomato producing small, apricot-coloured fruits with a lively sweet-tart flavour. Good for salads, roasting and drying.

fields.name: Marmande
fields.description: A traditional French beefsteak type with flattened, lightly ribbed red fruits. Often grown as a productive bush or semi-bush style tomato for salads and cooking.

fields.name: Mortgage Lifter
fields.description: A large pink beefsteak heirloom associated with Depression-era garden lore. Fruits can be very large, mild and meaty, making it a classic slicing tomato.

fields.name: Orange Banana
fields.description: An elongated orange paste tomato with dense flesh and bright colour. Useful for sauces, drying and adding a warm colour contrast to preserved tomato mixes.

fields.name: Pineapple
fields.description: A large yellow-orange beefsteak with red marbling inside the fruit. Sweet, low-acid flavour and dramatic colouring make it a useful showpiece variety.

fields.name: Principe Borghese
fields.description: A traditional Italian drying tomato producing clusters of small red fruits. Often grown for sun-drying, hanging trusses and concentrated tomato flavour.

fields.name: Riesentraube
fields.description: A prolific red cherry tomato whose name means giant bunch of grapes. Produces large clusters of small fruits with a bright, sweet-tart flavour.

fields.name: San Marzano
fields.description: A famous Italian plum tomato associated with sauce-making and preserving. Long red fruits have dense flesh and relatively few seeds, making them well suited to passata.

fields.name: Tigerella
fields.description: A striped salad tomato with red-orange fruits and golden markings. Early, decorative and useful for showing striped heritage-style tomatoes in the Seed Library.

fields.name: Yellow Pear
fields.description: A small pear-shaped yellow tomato grown for its cheerful colour and mild flavour. Good for salads, garnishes and introducing children to unusual tomato shapes.
